title: Suspend and resume lifecycle transitions
purpose: Mark a workflow's residency Suspended when it blocks on a durable wait and Resident when woken. Residency is an engine-runtime liveness flag, ORTHOGONAL to the aion-core WorkflowStatus (which has no Suspended variant and stays Running until a terminal event). AE owns these transitions around a durable wait; the durable-wait mechanism itself (timers, signal arrival) is AT's. This brief is the seam where the two meet.
task: Add a Residency field (Resident | Suspended) to the registry WorkflowHandle (registry/handle.rs) and implement lifecycle/transition.rs: suspend, which sets a registered handle's residency to Suspended when its workflow process blocks on a durable wait, and resume, which returns it to Resident when the awaited timer fires or signal arrives (or when AD replays it after restart). Residency is distinct from the handle's projected WorkflowStatus: suspend/resume NEVER change the projected status (a suspended workflow is still Running per the aion-core projection — there is no Suspended WorkflowStatus), and status reconciliation against the event projection (CO10) never touches residency, so there is no ordering race between them. Scope is the residency transitions only; the timer/signal machinery that triggers them is AT, the replay that re-creates a suspended workflow on restart is AD.

requirements.title: Suspend transition
requirements.spec: WHEN a workflow process blocks on a durable wait, THE SYSTEM SHALL set its handle's residency to Suspended in the registry, leaving the projected WorkflowStatus unchanged. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT remove the handle on suspend.
acceptance: registry/handle.rs gains a residency: Residency field (Residency = Resident | Suspended), distinct from the handle's status: WorkflowStatus; Residency is engine-internal and is NOT an aion-core type.

transition.rs provides suspend(id, run) that sets the handle's residency to Suspended and keeps the handle in the registry; it does NOT change the projected WorkflowStatus (which remains Running — there is no Suspended WorkflowStatus variant, and CO10 forbids inventing one).

Status reconciliation against the event projection (D3/CO10) only ever corrects the projected WorkflowStatus and never reads or writes residency, so a reconcile running before AT records a timer/signal event cannot revert the suspension.
lifecycle/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.

A test suspends a running handle and asserts it is still present with residency Suspended and projected status Running; a second test runs a reconcile after suspend and asserts residency stays Suspended.
IF the handle is absent, suspend returns EngineError::WorkflowNotFound.

requirements.title: Resume transition
requirements.spec: WHEN the durable wait completes (timer fired or signal arrived) or the workflow is replayed after a restart, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the handle's residency to Resident, leaving the projected WorkflowStatus unchanged.
acceptance: transition.rs provides resume(id, run) that sets the handle's residency back to Resident for a Suspended handle; it does not touch the projected WorkflowStatus.
resume is idempotent on an already-Resident handle (no error, no double effect).
A test suspends then resumes a handle and asserts the final residency is Resident.
IF the handle is absent, resume returns EngineError::WorkflowNotFound.

requirements.title: Durable-wait mechanism delegated to AT
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the durable timer, signal-arrival, or replay mechanism that triggers suspend/resume; it SHALL only own the registry residency transition, documenting the AT (timers/signals) and AD (replay) seams.
acceptance: transition.rs contains no timer-wheel scheduling, no signal-routing, and no replay logic.
Module docs state that suspend is invoked by AT when a workflow enters a durable wait and resume by AT/AD when the wait completes or the workflow is replayed.
grep of transition.rs shows no store-timer call and no mailbox/signal routing construct.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement durable timers or signal routing — those are AT.
SHALL NOT implement replay/recovery-on-startup — that is AD (it calls resume after re-creating a suspended workflow).
SHALL NOT append events — suspend/resume are live-view transitions; the durable record of a wait is the timer/signal event AT appends.
verification: suspend/resume only mutate registry residency (never the projected WorkflowStatus); reconciliation never touches residency (CO10 preserved); no AT/AD mechanism is implemented here.
